//: Contact registry (Phase 10b, presentation_layer_narrative.md §4.2). contact_id -> display metadata for the Burner Phone's threaded messages. 'unknown' is the reserved fallback thread for events an authored batch never tags with "contact" -- always present (ContactRegistry synthesizes it even if a future edit drops this row). The named contacts group the shipped gritty events (Sonnet 5, 10b tagging pass): coach_reyes carries the on-field/clubhouse arc, agent_diaz carries league/legal/health correspondence, girlfriend_jess carries the pro-era romance/marriage/family arc (age > 20), family_mom carries the pre-16 heir-interest beats. family_mom + family_dad are the HS-2 household threads -- CreateAvatar now inserts two real parent NPC Players rows; the HS-5 hs_dating_events.json batch finally gives family_dad content (the parental-approval beat). hs_sweetheart is HS-5's own pre-marriage dating-funnel thread (role: partner, gender-neutral) -- deliberately distinct from girlfriend_jess so the two romance arcs (HS vs pro) never share a contact thread or collide on the met_someone age>20 gate. The syndicate/arrest arc stays on 'unknown' by design -- those events are deliberately anonymous threats, not named relationships. family_kid is child_rearing_events.json's thread (arc-agnostic -- shared by HS- and pro-era children alike, since hs_has_child is set by both hs_baby_born and child_is_born) for the household's day-to-day parenting beats and the school's own calls home. coach_hs (Coach Malone) and hs_friend (Marcus) are the two new HS onboarding threads (hs_onboarding_events.json) -- coach_hs is the HS varsity coach, deliberately distinct from coach_reyes (the pro clubhouse coach) exactly as hs_sweetheart is kept distinct from girlfriend_jess, so the HS and pro arcs never share a contact thread; hs_friend is the classmate/buddy voice for the social/rival/homecoming beats. getting_started (docs/design/day1_phone_tutorial.md, Sonnet 5 Stage 2) is the pinned mechanical 'how to play' thread carrying the 4-event tutorial batch (getting_started_events.json) -- deliberately distinct from every narrative contact so coaching copy never pollutes a relationship thread. The design doc proposed a new role:'system'; ContactJson's role parser is a closed switch (throws on an unrecognized string), so per the doc's own contingency this ships on role:'unknown' instead -- zero ContactRegistry.cs touch.
